candidates.comparison: check_outputs_equal: exact token ID list == and exact string ==
candidates.oracle: HF greedy vs vLLM greedy, exact text/token match
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES — exact text and token ID equality (==) between two different implementations
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES — PyTorch numeric changes (kernel updates, decomposition changes) can shift logits enough to flip greedy argmax, especially at 5 max_tokens with long prompts
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES — HF and vLLM are independent implementations with different attention backends, different memory management, and different execution paths; no contract guarantees identical greedy output

candidates.comparison: check_outputs_equal: exact token ID list == and exact string ==
candidates.oracle: HF greedy vs vLLM greedy (tensor_parallel_size=2), exact text/token match
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES — exact text and token ID equality between HF and distributed vLLM
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES — distributed execution adds all-reduce non-determinism and reduction order sensitivity; PyTorch numeric changes amplified by TP communication
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES — HF single-GPU vs vLLM tensor-parallel are fundamentally different execution paths with no identical-output contract
